channel.title: Andrew's Agent Picks
channel.description: A bounded, agent-curated reading feed for Andrew's Matter Inbox.

item.title: Home Bias: The Hidden Cost of Staying Close to Home
item.dc:creator: Fabricio Oliveira, CFA
item.description: Why Andrew may want to read this: A clean portfolio-construction case on separating familiar risk from compensated risk, with useful implications for global allocation and client advice. Lane: financial. Agent relevance score: 88/100.

item.title: The Labor Market Explains Why Inflation Won’t Go Away
item.dc:creator: Torsten Slok
item.description: Why Andrew may want to read this: A concise, falsifiable macro thesis linking a historically long period below estimated NAIRU to sticky inflation and a higher-for-longer policy path. Lane: macro. Agent relevance score: 84/100.

item.title: Kimi K3, and what we can still learn from the pelican benchmark
item.dc:creator: Simon Willison
item.description: Why Andrew may want to read this: A timely model-evaluation case that distinguishes benchmark theater from practical evidence on cost, reasoning effort, tool use, and long-horizon agent performance. Lane: Ai-agents. Agent relevance score: 91/100.

item.title: Have your agent record video demos of its work with shot-scraper video
item.dc:creator: Simon Willison
item.description: Why Andrew may want to read this: A concrete agent-workflow pattern: have coding agents produce short, reviewable video demos from storyboard YAML. Useful for improving verification and handoff in the personal-agent buildout. Lane: Ai-agents. Agent relevance score: 92/100.

item.title: The Mythical Agent-Minute
item.dc:creator: Byrne Hobart
item.description: Why Andrew may want to read this: A sharp warning about measuring agentic work with seductive proxy metrics. Useful for designing the Matter pilot and broader agent scorecards around outcomes rather than activity. Lane: thinking. Agent relevance score: 88/100.

item.title: Blind Extrapolation as a Powerful Force in Finance
item.dc:creator: Byrne Hobart
item.description: Why Andrew may want to read this: Connects backward-looking investor expectations with excess volatility and shows how agentic coding makes market simulation faster. Relevant to both investment judgment and practical AI leverage. Lane: financial. Agent relevance score: 85/100.
